hero.heading: MILAN ROTHER
hero.tagline: I build simulation tools end-to-end. Numerical methods, infrastructure, and the interfaces to use them.

about.heading: WHO AM I
paragraphs: I build simulation infrastructure and the tools to use it. The numerics, the methods, the implementation, and the UI.
Currently consulting for MIT Plasma Science & Fusion Center, building the simulation stack for nuclear fusion fuel-cycle modeling.

M.Sc. Electrical Engineering. Deep background in numerical methods, dynamical systems, and scientific computing. My work spans from ODE solvers and EM methods in C and Rust to full browser-based engineering tools in SvelteKit.
PathSim is my open platform for dynamical system modeling. Collaborating with JSBSim, scikit-rf, python-control, and MIT PSFC. Check LinkedIn to see what I'm up to.

projects.heading: PROJECTS
projects.intro: Building open-source infrastructure for system modeling and simulation.

items.heading: PathSim
paragraphs: A pure-Python framework for modeling and simulating dynamical systems. Built from first principles with custom solvers, an event system, and a modular block-diagram API.
Published in JOSS. Adopted by JSBSim for flight dynamics and used at MIT PSFC for nuclear fusion fuel-cycle modeling.

items.heading: PathView
paragraphs: A browser-based visual editor for PathSim models. Drag-and-drop block diagrams, live simulation preview, and instant sharing. Built with SvelteKit, SvelteFlow, and Pyodide.

items.heading: PySimHub
paragraphs: An open community catalog bridging Python's scattered simulation communities. An awesome list that's also awesome to use.

items.heading: RapidPassives
paragraphs: RFIC passive design tool. Browser-based layout generation with real-time preview, GDS export, and integrated MOM solver.

services.heading: SERVICES
services.intro: I help engineering teams answer strategic questions and build features in the simulation modeling and scientific UI/UX domain.

categories.heading: // simulation infrastructure
categories.text: Custom solvers, model architectures, and simulation pipelines. From numerical methods to deployment. Python, C, Rust, WASM. Including Simulink migration to open-source.

categories.heading: // scientific UI/UX
categories.text: Browser-based tools for engineering workflows. Interactive visualization, model editors, and dashboards. SvelteKit, TypeScript, Pyodide.

categories.heading: // PathSim integration & support
categories.text: Adopt PathSim for your domain. Custom blocks, solver tuning, toolbox development. Ongoing support contracts available.

categories.heading: // digital twin & co-simulation
categories.text: FMU/FMI-compliant architectures connecting simulation models to real-time sensor data and control systems. Standards-based interoperability across platforms.

categories.heading: // training & workshops
categories.text: Hands-on Python simulation workshops for your team. From basics to advanced system modeling. 1-2 days, remote or on-site.

services.closing: I scope work to weekly sprints and multi-week projects. Let's talk about what you need.
